title: quercus robur var. brachybalana
intro: Quercus robur var. brachybalana, also known as the Pedunculate Oak, is a lesser-known variety of the Quercus robur tree, native to parts of Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Quercus robur var. brachybalana include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to support overall health and well-being, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to limited research and potential interactions with medications.
